title: new Sequelize with dialectOptions.ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the database TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: Sequelize validates the database server certificate (dialectOptions.ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new Sequelize is created with dialectOptions: { ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } }, disabling validation of the database server's TLS certificate for every connection in the pool. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database — exposing every query, credential, and row. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the managed provider's CA via dialectOptions.ssl.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No Sequelize construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: Sequelize does not set dialectOptions.ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: dialectOptions: { ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } } is the most-copied Sequelize snippet for connecting to Heroku/RDS Postgres without the CA. It works, so it ships — silently disabling MITM protection on the database.
